name: Do general contractors use subcontractors, and should I be worried about that?
headline: Do general contractors use subcontractors, and should I be worried about that?

mainEntity.name: Do general contractors use subcontractors, and should I be worried about that?
mainEntity.text: Do general contractors use subcontractors, and should I be worried about that?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Bay Area, it’s common for general contractors to use subcontractors for specialized work like electrical, plumbing, HVAC, roofing, stucco, and sometimes drywall or painting. The general contractor stays responsible for managing the subs, scheduling, and making sure their work fits the overall plan and passes inspections. This is normal and not a bad thing. Many subs do the same type of work every day and are very skilled. What matters is how the general contractor chooses and supervises them. You can ask how long they’ve worked with their subs, whether the subs are licensed where required, and who will be on site each day. Your main contact should always be the general contractor or their project manager, not each individual sub. If there’s a problem with a plumber or electrician, you tell the general contractor and they handle it. A pitfall is when a contractor disappears and leaves you dealing with subs directly. Make sure your contract clearly states that the general contractor is responsible for all work and pays the subs. You should only be paying the general contractor, according to the schedule you agreed on.
